featured.title: From $40k in Debt to Lean FIRE at 33
featured.subtitle: The real journey from student loan debt to living lean and free by 33
featured.description: How I went from $40,000 in student loan debt to Lean FIRE at 33: the high income, the untouchable savings habit, a 50% savings rate, and how long it really took.

featured.title: My First Year of Lean FIRE: The Real Experience
featured.subtitle: What the first 12 months of a small and free life abroad actually looked like
featured.description: An honest first person account of my first year of Lean FIRE: leaving burnout in New York, landing in Da Nang, and building a free life on $1,100 to $1,800 a month.

featured.title: Is Lean FIRE Right For You?
featured.subtitle: Six honest questions I worked through before I leaned into a small and free life
featured.description: Lean FIRE is about far more than a savings number. Here are the 6 honest questions I worked through before retiring lean in Vietnam, from securing health costs to knowing how you will fill your days.

featured.title: Retiring in Southeast Asia as an Indian: The Honest Math
featured.subtitle: Vietnam and Thailand vs Pune — where the money actually goes, and the one lever nobody tells you about
featured.description: An Indian's real cost of retiring in Southeast Asia: Vietnam and Thailand vs Pune, visas on an Indian passport, and why the money is a wash but the life isn't.

featured.title: The Hard Truth About Passive Income: Why The Internet Is Lying To You
featured.subtitle: Passive income is real, but the way it is sold to you is a catastrophic misrepresentation of the suffering required to achieve it.
featured.description: An honest, hyper-personal breakdown of the four types of income, the nomadic journey, and why trying to separate your time from your money is the hardest active grind you will ever face.

featured.title: Do Not Pursue FIRE If You Cannot Pass This Honest Test
featured.subtitle: A harsh, psychological reality check on whether you actually want early retirement, or if you just hate your job and love spending money.
featured.description: FIRE is not for everyone. Find out if your relationship with money, possessions, and purpose will make early retirement a dream or a living nightmare.

featured.title: What Is Lean FIRE? My Real $1,800/Month Life in Vietnam
featured.subtitle: The plain-English definition, the math, and exactly where my money goes
featured.description: Lean FIRE means retiring early on $20K–$40K a year. Here's the full definition, how to calculate your Lean FIRE number, and my real $1,800/month example living in Vietnam.

featured.title: How much do you need to retire at 40?
featured.subtitle: A detailed breakdown of the math behind early retirement
featured.description: See how much money you need to retire at 40 using annual expenses, safe withdrawal rates, bridge accounts, and realistic savings targets.

featured.title: Lean FIRE vs Fat FIRE vs Barista FIRE vs Coast FIRE
featured.subtitle: And which is the right one for you?
featured.description: Compare Lean FIRE, Fat FIRE, Barista FIRE, and Coast FIRE so you can choose the financial independence path that fits your life.

featured.title: How to Calculate Your FIRE Number
featured.subtitle: A practical walkthrough of the 4% rule, the India 33× rule, and how to personalize your number
featured.description: How to calculate your FIRE number in dollars or rupees: the 25× (4%) rule for the US, the safer 33× (3%) rule for India, real examples in ₹/crores, and a free calculator.

recent.title: The 3 Buckets I Use to Fund Early Retirement (Warts and All)
recent.subtitle: A CD ladder, an equities engine, and a locked backstop — the real, imperfect portfolio structure behind my FIRE
recent.description: The 3-bucket strategy I use to fund early retirement: a ~10-year CD ladder, an equities growth bucket, and a locked 401(k) backstop. Percentages, real trade-offs, and how it survived a 3.5% year.

recent.title: Living in Vietnam on an Indian Passport: My Real Visa Setup
recent.subtitle: The e-visa, the port-of-entry rule, and how I actually handle visa runs — from someone living it in Da Nang
recent.description: How Indians get a Vietnam visa and stay long-term: the official e-visa ($25 single / $50 multiple, up to 90 days), the port-of-entry rule, and real visa-run options — first-hand from an Indian living in Da Nang.

recent.title: What Breast Cancer Cost Us in India — and What It Did to My FIRE Plan
recent.subtitle: The real, itemised out-of-pocket cost of a triple-negative breast cancer treatment in Pune — and what a ₹40-lakh bill does to a retirement plan.
recent.description: The real, itemised cost of breast cancer treatment in India without insurance (Pune, 2026): chemotherapy, immunotherapy, surgery, radiation and scans — close to ₹40 lakh — plus how we cut the bill and what it did to a FIRE plan.

recent.title: This one call helped me retire 4 years faster
recent.subtitle: 4 things I did which helped me make money quicker and fast track my way into an early retirement at just 33.
recent.description: How one compensation negotiation added $200K to my offer and helped speed up my path to early retirement.

recent.title: The Painful Psychology of Why You Won't Negotiate Your Next Job Offer
recent.subtitle: And how this psychological trap could cost you $500,000+ over your career
recent.description: A breakdown of the cognitive biases—loss aversion, risk aversion, and the authority trap—that cause 55% of workers to accept the first number on the table.

recent.title: Can I Retire at 35 With $500k?
recent.subtitle: Every strategy simulated — withdrawal plans, real estate, dividends, and the geo-arbitrage path an NRI actually used to FIRE in Southeast Asia. Just math.
recent.description: Can you retire at 35 with $500k (~₹4.7 crore)? Every strategy run with real math — withdrawal rates, dividends, real estate, and the geo-arbitrage route an NRI used to FIRE in Southeast Asia.

recent.title: Do You Actually Need to Retire?
recent.subtitle: What if the question isn't 'how do I retire early' — but 'why do I want to escape my life?'
recent.description: What if retirement isn't the answer? A deeply personal exploration of what retirement really means, why we chase it, whether it's worth it, and what happens when you get there.

recent.title: Living Abroad on $1,100 a Month
recent.subtitle: How I cut my expenses by 82% without sacrificing quality of life — and the honest all-in number
recent.description: A real, line-by-line breakdown of how I went from $6,000/month in NYC to a ~$1,100 core cost of living in Da Nang, Vietnam — plus the honest $1,800 all-in number most geo-arbitrage posts hide.
